title: Long-Range Dependence in Financial Markets: Empirical Evidence and Generative Modeling Challenges
abstract: arXiv:2509.19663v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: This study provides an empirical investigation of long-range dependence (LRD) in financial markets and evaluates the ability of deep generative models to reproduce such temporal structures. Using daily data from three sectors--equity (S&P 500, DAX, Nikkei 225), commodities (Wheat, Corn, Soybeans), and energy (UNG, USO, XLE)--we examine LRD through rescaled range (R/S) analysis, detrended fluctuation analysis (DFA), segmented multifractal analysis around the COVID-19 period, and an ARFIMA--FIGARCH model with Student's $t$-distributed innovations. The evidence suggests that while mean returns exhibit limited persistence, pronounced long memory is observed in conditional volatility across most assets, and equity-market scaling properties change non-negligibly after 2020. Building on these findings, we assess whether Quant Generative Adversarial Networks (Quant GANs) can learn and reproduce these stylized temporal dependencies against ec

Title: Long-Range Dependence in Financial Markets: Empirical Evidence and Generative Modeling Challenges
Abstract: arXiv:2509.19663v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: This study provides an empirical investigation of long-range dependence (LRD) in financial markets and evaluates the ability of deep generative models to reproduce such temporal structures. Using daily data from three sectors--equity (S&P 500, DAX, Nikkei 225), commodities (Wheat, Corn, Soybeans), and energy (UNG, USO, XLE)--we examine LRD through rescaled range (R/S) analysis, detrended fluctuation analysis (DFA), segmented multifractal analysis around the COVID-19 period, and an ARFIMA--FIGARCH model with Student's $t$-distributed innovations. The evidence suggests that while mean returns exhibit limited persistence, pronounced long memory is observed in conditional volatility across most assets, and equity-market scaling properties change non-negligibly after 2020. Building on these findings, we assess whether Quant Generative Adversarial Networks (Quant GANs) can learn and reproduce these stylized temporal dependencies against ec
translated_title: 金融市场中的长程依赖性：实证证据与生成建模挑战
translated_abstract: 本研究对金融市场中的长程依赖性进行了实证调查，并评估了深度生成模型再现此类时间结构的能力。利用来自三个板块的日度数据——股票（标普500、德国DAX指数、日经225指数）、大宗商品（小麦、玉米、大豆）和能源（UNG、USO、XLE）——我们通过重标极差分析、去趋势波动分析、围绕COVID-19时期的分段多重分形分析以及具有学生t分布创新项的ARFIMA-FIGARCH模型来检验长程依赖性。证据表明，虽然均值收益率表现出有限的持续性，但大多数资产的条件波动率中观察到显著的长记忆性，且2020年后股票市场的标度特性发生了不可忽略的变化。基于这些发现，我们评估了量化生成对抗网络在生态学意义上学习并再现这些程式化的时间依赖性的能力。
tldr: 本研究实证发现金融市场中波动率存在显著长记忆性，且2020年后股票市场标度特性发生变化，并检验了深度生成模型再现这些时间结构的能力。
en_tdlr: This study empirically finds significant long memory in financial market volatility and changes in equity-market scaling properties after 2020, while evaluating deep generative models' ability to reproduce these temporal structures.
